summary: Iteration 2 backend testing complete. 19/19 new tests passed: featured stones flag + public stones endpoint, public render permalink, public quote submission with visualization linkage, admin quote list/filter/PATCH/DELETE with RBAC, Google auth error paths. Regression suite also re-run (16/16 after fixing one stale legacy test that still used method='stripe'). One real end-to-end Gemini visualize call succeeded inside the iteration2 fixture.

minor.issue: Duplicate class definitions: QuoteCreate and QuoteUpdate are each defined TWICE. The first QuoteUpdate also has a stray orphan field `session_id: str` inserted after the original body (likely an editor merge artifact from the GoogleAuthReq class). The duplicates shadow the originals at import time so endpoints function correctly, but this is dead/broken code that should be cleaned up.

action_items: Clean up duplicate QuoteCreate/QuoteUpdate class definitions and the orphan `session_id: str` field in /app/backend/server.py lines 173-191 (cosmetic; functionality not impacted because the second definition shadows the first).
critical_code_review_comments: server.py has duplicate Pydantic class blocks (QuoteCreate, QuoteUpdate) plus an orphan `session_id: str` that landed inside the first QuoteUpdate class body. Remove the redundant block (lines ~177-191). Risk: a future Pydantic strict mode or someone reordering the file could expose the orphan required field and break PATCH /api/admin/quotes/{id}.

CORS uses allow_origins=['*'] with allow_credentials=False — fine for current public API surface, but if cookie-based auth is added later (e.g. for Google OAuth callback), this will need an explicit origin allowlist.
create_quote stores client IP from request.client.host without rate-limiting. Since /api/quotes is public and unauthenticated, consider adding basic rate limit / captcha to avoid lead-form spam.
PATCH /api/admin/stones/{id} permits arbitrary featured toggles without validating that boolean (Pydantic does coerce). Acceptable.
Public render permalink correctly strips _id and password_hash style fields; result_image is a data URL (large) — fine for MVP but consider serving a smaller thumbnail field for SEO/sharing previews.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_rated_worktops.py (fixed stale stripe-based purchase tests to use paypal — stripe is no longer an accepted method)
test_credentials: Admin: admin@ratedworktops.com / Admin@RW2026 (verified working). Fresh users created per test run via /api/auth/register.
seed_data_creation: Each test run creates: 1 fresh user (it2_*@ratedworktops.com), 1 visualization via real Gemini call, 3 quote rows (2 standard + 1 deleted). Stones state mutates `featured` flag on carrara-white (reset to true at end).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 2 fully green. New test file /app/backend/tests/test_iteration2.py covers public/stones+featured filter, public/renders/{id}, public POST /quotes (with visualization linkage), admin GET/PATCH/DELETE /admin/quotes with RBAC, and /auth/google error paths. The fixture `visualization_id` makes a real Gemini call (~3-8s) — keep timeout >= 120s if reusing. The only known code smell is duplicate class blocks at server.py lines ~177-191 (does not break anything currently). Stripe is intentionally rejected by /api/credits/purchase per iteration 2 spec; only paypal/apple_pay/google_pay are valid (all MOCKED).
rca of the issue: N/A — no failing flows. Single stale legacy assertion (purchase via stripe) was a test artifact, not a product bug; corrected to use paypal per current contract.
